task_completion.description: Open NASA Panoply and load the required NCEP climatology datasets.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The NASA Panoply GUI is open, and both 'prate.sfc.mon.ltm.nc' and 'air.mon.ltm.nc' are visible in the Datasets/Sources panel.

task_completion.description: Create a precipitation plot for the month of October.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window is visible displaying precipitation (prate) data, with the time/array index explicitly set to October (e.g., time step 10 or labeled as October).

task_completion.description: Create a surface air temperature plot for the month of October.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window is visible displaying surface air temperature (air) data, with the time/array index explicitly set to October.

task_completion.description: Export the two plot images to the LocustAssessment directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog in Panoply or a file manager/terminal window showing 'precip_october.png' and 'temperature_october.png' saved in the '~/Documents/LocustAssessment/' directory.

task_completion.description: Create a structured assessment report identifying the optimal breeding zones.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., gedit, nano) is open showing 'breeding_report.txt' being saved to '~/Documents/LocustAssessment/'. The text must identify optimal breeding zones such as the Horn of Africa, Arabian Peninsula, Somalia, Yemen, or Oman.

integrity.description: The agent used the NASA Panoply GUI to generate the plots rather than using a script to bypass the visual tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Panoply graphical interface being actively interacted with to create and view the plots.

integrity.description: The agent manually created the breeding report based on visual assessment rather than using a script to hallucinate or copy-paste the answer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or editing 'breeding_report.txt' in a standard text editor, and the Panoply plots were viewed prior to or during the creation of the report.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply move pre-existing files or download the answers from an external source.
integrity.visual_evidence: The progression of screenshots shows the step-by-step creation of the plots in Panoply and the drafting of the text report.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The correct breeding zones for October, based on the intersection of optimal temperatures (20-38 C) and residual precipitation (>1mm/day) in the NCEP data, are the Horn of Africa, Arabian Peninsula, East Africa, Somalia, Yemen, and Oman. The expected output files are 'precip_october.png', 'temperature_october.png', and 'breeding_report.txt', all of which must be located in '~/Documents/LocustAssessment/'.
